column8: Julia and her mum and dad were about to go on their summer holiday. Julia______________forward to it at all. (NOT LOOK)
column9: They were staying in the same cottage as last year. And last year, they'd seen a number of_________inside the house. (MOUSE)
column10: Julia didn't like them at all. She thought they were the_______________creatures on Earth. (FRIGHTENING)
column11: Julia had seen a small one in the kitchen, and another large one in her bedroom. When she saw them, she____________out of the house as fast as she could. (RUN)
column12: "I promise you I'll go through the house and make sure there____________none there before we go in," said Julia's dad. (BE)
column13: "How will you do that, Dad?" What if they____________in the walls?" Julia asked. (LIVE)
column14: Julia's dad____________that she was right. It was impossible to be certain. (KNOW)
column15: Then, he had an idea. "I know!" he said. "Let's ask_________lovely neighbour Mrs Anderson if we can borrow one of her cats to take with us". (WE)
column16: "That's great idea!" said Julia. "I'd feel a lot______________with Tiger or Monty there to chase them away!" (SAFE)
column17: The River Volga is Russian's main river. It's also the____________river in both Russia and Europe. (LARGE)
column18: It is situated in the western part of Russia and____________through the country from north to south, emptying into the Caspian Sea. (GO)
column19: Over the centuries, the river____________a very important role in the history of Russia and Europe. (PLAY)
column20: It once served as a trade route that_____________southern Russia and Scandinavia. (CONNECT)
column21: The river was widened in the 20th century so that ships could travel through_________waters more easily. (IT)
column22: Currently, ships from other counties______________to travel through the river. This means only Russian ships can do so. (NOT ALLOW)
column23: Several artists______________scenes of the river over the years, many of which hang in museums and galleries. (PAINT)
column24: Many Russian writers have fallen in love with the River Volga.___________stories often describe the river as place of fascination, legends and traditional. (THEY)
column25: US musician Glenn Miller______________a folk songs about the Volga. He loved it so much, he decided to do his own jazz version, which was a hit in America. (HEAR)
